title: Aureline target-graph snapshot, workset-scope binding, toolchain/build-system fingerprint, and export/replay linkage contract
description: Boundary schema for the target_graph_snapshot_record every build adapter (native runner, Build Server Protocol client, Bazel Build Event Protocol reader, structured importer, heuristic target inferrer, fallback opaque-preservation adapter) emits and every consumer (run / test / build / debug picker, quick-open, command palette, CI overlay, AI planner, support-bundle exporter, replay harness) reads to know one stable graph identity, one workset_scope binding, one source-adapter id, one toolchain / build-system fingerprint, one captured-at timestamp, one freshness posture, and one export / replay linkage. Pickers, CI overlays, and AI planners cite a graph_id when they reference a target; the same target_id resolved against the same graph_id resolves the same target across export and reopen. Lower-confidence adapters MUST NOT silently replace higher-confidence adapters' graphs; replacements ride an adapter_replacement_event_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) whose visible_state_change_class is non-null. Sparse / partial-workset graphs MUST cite a partial_truth_class so consumers never render a partial graph as a full graph. This schema does not mint a new confidence vocabulary, a new scope vocabulary, a new target-class vocabulary, or a new toolchain-class vocabulary; it re-exports the frozen tokens from schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json (confidence_class) and schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json (scope_class). Adding a new freshness_class, partial_truth_class, fingerprint_kind, replay_linkage_class, generation_event_class, or graph_lineage_class is additive-minor and bumps target_graph_snapshot_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

target_graph_snapshot_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the target-graph snapshot contract. Current value is 1.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log and export. Raw secrets, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

confidence_class.description: Re-export of schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json#/$defs/confidence_class. The graph's confidence is the source-adapter's confidence projected onto this snapshot; consumers MUST NOT raise it above the adapter ceiling.

freshness_class.description: Closed graph-freshness vocabulary. live_streaming is reserved for protocols that publish updates as targets change (BSP buildTargetsChanged, BEP streaming). support_bundle_replay_only is reserved for graphs replayed from a support bundle; consumers MUST NOT invoke actions against these without re-resolving in the active context.

partial_truth_class.description: Closed partial-truth vocabulary. complete_for_workset_scope means the graph fully covers the bound workset_scope. Any partial_* class MUST be rendered as a partial graph by every consumer; pickers MUST disclose the partial-coverage chip and MUST NOT silently advertise targets that fall outside the cited slice as missing.

fingerprint_kind.description: Closed fingerprint-kind vocabulary. Snapshots cite at least one toolchain_identity_fingerprint and one build_system_version_fingerprint (or heuristic_ruleset_fingerprint for heuristic adapters); consumers compare fingerprints to decide whether two snapshots reference comparable build truth.

fingerprint_digest.description: Hash-algorithm-prefixed digest of the fingerprint inputs. Raw lockfile contents, raw env values, raw secrets, and raw paths never appear here; producers retain inputs out-of-band and cite this digest.

replay_linkage_class.description: Closed replay-linkage vocabulary. Snapshots that participate in a task-event replay bundle, support bundle, or import-probe export cite that linkage so consumers can re-resolve target ids from a replayed graph rather than minting new ids.

task_event_replay_bundle_ref.description: Ref to the task_event_replay_bundle_record (in schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json) the snapshot belongs to. MUST be non-null when linkage_class is linked_to_task_event_replay_bundle.

generation_event_class.description: Closed generation-event vocabulary naming why this snapshot was generated. CI overlays and AI planners read this so a snapshot tagged after_user_invoked_refresh is treated as fresher than a snapshot tagged after_support_bundle_replay even when their captured_at timestamps are similar.

graph_lineage_class.description: Closed graph-lineage vocabulary. supersedes_prior_snapshot_after_adapter_swap MUST cite a target_graph_lineage_record citing the swap's adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.

graph_id.description: Stable graph id. Surfaces cite this id alongside a target_id when they reference a target; the same target_id under the same graph_id resolves the same target across export and reopen.

workset_scope_ref.description: Ref to the workset_scope object in schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json the snapshot was resolved under. The snapshot does not re-mint scope vocabulary; it cites the scope record.

execution_context_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the execution_context_record this snapshot was resolved against. Null only when the snapshot is a static-import probe with no live execution context.

source_adapter_id_ref.description: Ref to the adapter_descriptor_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) that emitted this snapshot.

source_adapter_confidence_class.description: Confidence projected onto this snapshot. MUST NOT exceed the source adapter's adapter_confidence_ceiling.

predecessor_graph_id_ref.description: Ref to the predecessor graph_id this snapshot supersedes (or coexists alongside). MUST be non-null when graph_lineage_class is supersedes_prior_snapshot_same_adapter / supersedes_prior_snapshot_after_adapter_swap / coexists_side_by_side_with_higher_precedence_snapshot / supersedes_prior_snapshot_with_target_set_change / deprecated_replaced_by_new_graph_id.

adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.description: Ref to the adapter_replacement_event_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) when graph_lineage_class is supersedes_prior_snapshot_after_adapter_swap. MUST be non-null in that case so the visible state change is traceable.

target_descriptor_refs.description: Refs to target_descriptor_records (in schemas/tooling/target_descriptor.schema.json) published under this graph. Empty for a snapshot that publishes only the partial-truth posture (for example partial_initial_load_pending_full_resolution before any target resolves).

target_count_reported.description: Reported number of target descriptors this snapshot publishes. MUST equal the length of target_descriptor_refs when partial_truth_class is complete_for_workset_scope; MAY be greater than target_descriptor_refs.length when partial_truth_class indicates an in-flight load.

stale_after_ttl.description: ISO 8601 duration after which the snapshot is treated as stale_pending_refresh by the freshness contract. Null when the adapter does not publish a TTL.

target_graph_snapshot_record.description: One target-graph snapshot. Pickers, quick-open, run / test / build / debug surfaces, CI overlays, AI planners, support-bundle exporters, and replay harnesses cite this record rather than mint a parallel graph object.

adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.description: Ref to the adapter_replacement_event_record when lineage_class is supersedes_prior_snapshot_after_adapter_swap; MUST be non-null in that case.

target_id_continuity.description: Names whether target ids minted under prior_graph_ids survive into current_graph_id. Pickers and AI planners read this to decide whether to re-render a target list silently or to render a target_set_narrowed / target_set_widened review chip.

target_graph_lineage_record.description: Records lineage events between two or more graph_ids. Pickers, AI planners, CI overlays, and support-bundle exporters read these so a graph rotation never appears as a silent disappearance plus reappearance.

allOf.description: first_snapshot_no_predecessor lineage MUST carry a null predecessor_graph_id_ref and a null adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.
supersedes_prior_snapshot_* and coexists_* and deprecated_* lineage MUST carry a non-null predecessor_graph_id_ref.

complete_for_workset_scope MUST report target_count_reported equal to target_descriptor_refs.length and a non-empty descriptor list (or report zero only when the workset scope legitimately resolves to zero targets).
support_bundle_replay_only freshness MUST pair replay_linkage.linkage_class with linked_to_support_bundle or linked_to_task_event_replay_bundle.
linked_to_task_event_replay_bundle MUST carry a non-null task_event_replay_bundle_ref.
linked_to_support_bundle MUST carry a non-null support_bundle_ref.
live_no_replay_linkage MUST carry null refs across the linkage payload.
after_adapter_swap_visible_state_change generation MUST pair with supersedes_prior_snapshot_after_adapter_swap lineage.
Lineage events naming a non-first lineage_class MUST cite at least one prior_graph_ids entry.
first_snapshot_no_predecessor lineage events MUST carry an empty prior_graph_ids list.
